title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 13.
content:
There was an Old Man of Kilkenny,
    Who never had more than a penny;
    He spent all that money,
    In onions and honey,
    That wayward Old Man of Kilkenny.
meaning:
"Book Of Nonsense Limerick 13." is a quintessential example of Edward Lear's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
